concepts.title: Definition of Sports Nutrition
concepts.description: Sports nutrition refers to the science of using food and nutrients to improve athletic performance and enhance overall health and well-being.

concepts.title: Carbohydrates as the primary source of energy
concepts.description: Carbohydrates are the primary source of energy for the body, particularly for athletes and individuals who engage in regular physical activity.

concepts.title: Importance of Hydration
concepts.description: Proper hydration is essential for maintaining physical performance, preventing dehydration, and reducing the risk of heat-related illnesses.

concepts.title: MACRONUTRIENT Mnemonic
concepts.description: Use the mnemonic 'CARBS' to remember the three main macronutrients: Carbohydrates, Amino Acids (Protein), and Fats, and Refined Sugars (not essential but important to know)

concepts.title: Inadequate Recovery Nutrition
concepts.description: Many athletes make the mistake of not consuming adequate recovery nutrition after a workout or competition, which can lead to poor recovery, decreased performance, and increased risk of injury or illness.

concepts.title: Daily Energy Expenditure (DEE)
concepts.description: Daily Energy Expenditure (DEE) is the total amount of energy expended by the body in a 24-hour period, and it includes basal metabolic rate (BMR), thermic effect of food (TEF), and physical activity level (PAL).

concepts.title: Importance of Periodized Nutrition
concepts.description: Periodized nutrition involves adjusting an athlete's diet to match their training phase, which can help improve performance, enhance recovery, and reduce the risk of overreaching or overtraining.

concepts.title: Role of Antioxidants
concepts.description: Antioxidants play a crucial role in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can help improve athletic performance, reduce muscle damage, and prevent chronic diseases.
